sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 3.42 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.3)

statement.latex: (Kneser--Tits conjecture). Let$G$be a simply connected$k$-defined simple algebraic group, and$E_k(G)$the subgroup generated by unipotent$k$-elements. If$E_k(G) 1$, then$G_k=E_k(G)$. The proof is known for$k$-decomposable groups (C.\,Chevalley) and for local fields (V.\,P.\,Platonov). { V.\,P.\,Platonov} The conjecture was refuted (V.\,P.\,Platonov, , { 10}, no.\,2 (1976), 211--243).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
